entry.title: Latest People Profile Cards Configuration - Move to another category & RBP
entry.content:
Hello, 

I have two separate questions related to configuring the Latest People Profile in SAP SuccessFactors: 

  1. Is it possible to move predefined cards to another category? Currently Development Goals sits in the Learning and Development Category (and Performance Goals sits in the Performance and Goals category), but we want to put them in the same category (since our employees associate them together via the same page in the Goals Module). I see we have the option to move custom cards to different categories, but don't see the same option for predefined cards, is there a workaround?
  2. Where can I find the Role-Based-Permissions to grant visibility to other role groups to view the Learning History card (and data) and Required Learning card (and data) in the Learning and Development category? Currently the employee can see their own data in these cards, but we want to grant visibility to Managers and HR Partners. I've tried looking in Manage Role Permissions under "Learning", "Manage Learning", "Employee Widgets", Employee Views", "Employee Data" ... any other thoughts? Is this found in permissions within Learning Administration?

I'm not sure if it's relevant to call this out, but we do NOT have Employee Central, but we do have Performance Management/Goal Management, Learning, Success and Development.

Thank you for any help you can provide!
Geena

entry.title: Re: LOI Concept
entry.content:
Hi Vijay,

LOI - Letter of Intent is mostly used in Govt/PSU and Banking industries. For example, the organization wants to renew the insurance for employees for the new fiscal year. The insurance policy is expired on 31st March. So, the next renewal should be on 1st April. But the organization finalize to renew the insurance policy with the existing insurance company or any new company by 20th of March. As 20th March very near to 31st March and there is only 11 days in hand to prepare all required documents, organization may fail to issue the PO or Contract to the Insurance company before 31st March. To overcome such situation, the Organization will issue a LOI to the Insurance company for proceeding renew of emplyees insurance policy. The Insurance company will go ahead with the LOI and later the PO or Contract or Legal Documents will issue or signed by both partyes.

So, coming to SAP, we can use a new print output of PO/Contract as LOI - Letter of Intent. We can issue the LOI by giving the basic required input in PO/Contract and take print output as LOI. Later we can issue PO/Contract output after complete legal terms and conditions enter.

Thank You,

Ratiprakash Behera

entry.title: The HCM Ecosystem Is on a Roll and Sapphire Is Next
entry.content:
It has been a big few months for the SAP SuccessFactors partner community. From the energy on the floor in Las Vegas to over 1,050 partners gathering in Bangkok, the 2026 SAP Partner Summit series made one thing unmistakably clear: this ecosystem is firing on all cylinders.

Before we head into Sapphire, we wanted to take a moment to look back and share some highlights.

Bangkok and Las Vegas: What We Took Away

The theme across both summits was Together, We Elevate, and it showed. Customers are navigating real complexity: new technologies, evolving regulations, workforce expectations that are shifting faster than ever. And the partners making the biggest difference are the ones who show up not just with implementation skills, but with the kind of certified HCM expertise and regional depth that connects strategy to real business outcomes.

From the sessions in Las Vegas to the energy on the floor in Bangkok, the message was consistent. When partners and SAP SuccessFactors move together, customers win. It is what we heard from partners across every conversation at both events.

We also took time to recognize the partners raising the bar. Congratulations to all of the 2026 SAP Partner Award winners, including Deloitte for taking home HCM Excellence honors in Southeast Asia. The standard of work across this community continues to set the pace.

See It for Yourself

All of that energy, expertise, and partnership has been building for a long time. We captured some of it in this Partner Mashup video.

Join Us

With over 12,000 certified consultants across 100+ countries and 300+ certified partner apps, this is one of the most trusted partner ecosystems in HCM. And Sapphire is where we bring all of it together on the biggest stage of the year.

Sapphire — Virtual Join us online for the latest SAP SuccessFactors HCM innovations and hear how partners are helping customers realize the full value of their investment. Register now

entry.title: RAP: Fiori Elements List Report – How to sort fuzzy search results by relevance score?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working on a requirement using RAP (RESTful ABAP Programming Model) and facing a challenge related to search behavior.

Project Scenario

RAP application based on CDS View Entity

  • UI: Fiori Elements List Report
  • Search enabled using:
    • @search.searchable: true
    • Fuzzy search on material description
  • Backend system: S/4HANA / ABAP Cloud

The fuzzy search works correctly and returns matching records.

Problem

Although the search results are correct, they are not sorted by relevance (matching score).
I want the results to be sorted by how well they match the search text (best match on top).

I tried:

  • @Consumption.ranked: true
    but it does not work for my List Report scenario.

What I found

  • Blogs suggest using AMDP / Custom Entity to calculate search score.
  • However, in RAP:
    • I cannot access the search string entered in the UI
    • The fuzzy score is not exposed in CDS
  • I would prefer to keep a standard CDS-based RAP solution if possible.

My Questions

  1. Is there any standard or recommended way in RAP/CDS to:
    • access fuzzy search relevance score, or
    • sort results by relevance?
  2. Is Custom Entity with AMDP the only supported solution?
  3. Is SAP planning any enhancement in RAP to handle search ranking?
  4. Are there any best-practice alternatives used in real projects?

What I want to avoid

  • Frontend-only sorting
  • Hardcoding logic
  • Unclear or unsupported hacks

Any guidance, confirmation, or reference from SAP experts would be really helpful.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Kind regards,

Mohammad Afrad Husain.

entry.title: RAP : How do you model complex validations & error handling without breaking Fiori UX
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,
I would like to get guidance and best practices from the community and SAP experts.

Project Context

  • Technology: RAP (ABAP Cloud)
  • UI: Fiori Elements (Object Page + List Report)
  • Backend: S/4HANA
  • Business Object with:
    • Draft enabled
    • Multiple child entities
    • Complex business validations

Real Problem Statement

In our project, we have complex validations such as:

  • Cross‑entity checks (header + items)
  • Checks against existing records
  • Conditional validations based on status, user role, or business rules

We implemented validations using:

  • RAP validation methods
  • FAILED, REPORTED, and %msg handling

Technically, this works — but the user experience is not always optimal.

Issues We Are Facing

  • Errors sometimes appear late (only on Save)
  • Messages are valid but not contextual enough for business users
  • Handling multiple error messages becomes confusing
  • Some validations would ideally run earlier, not just on Save
  • We want to avoid:
    • Custom UI logic
    • Non‑standard hacks
    • Breaking RAP principles

Questions to the Community 

How do you design validations in RAP for complex business objects without flooding the user with errors?

  1. What is the recommended balance between:
    • RAP validations
    • Determinations
    • Frontend behavior (without custom UI code)?
  2. Do you follow any patterns for:
    • Grouping related validation messages
    • Showing errors closer to the field instead of generic messages?
  3. Are there real‑project best practices SAP recommends but are not well documented?

What We Want to Achieve

  • Clean RAP implementation
  • Predictable and user‑friendly Fiori behavior
  • Clear error messages that business users understand
  • Scalable design (future rules will be added)

kind regards,

Mohammad Afrad Husain.

entry.title: ABAP XFT - Upload PDF to File Node
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am currently working on an ABAP requirement where I need to programmatically attach a generated PDF document to an XFT node of type File.

Current implementation status:

  1. I generate the form output from SmartForms and receive the OTF data.
  2. I convert the OTF output into PDF format (XSTRING).
  3. I already have the header / identification data of the target XFT node.

My issue is with the final step: I am unable to find the correct standard function module, class, or API to attach the PDF binary content (XSTRING) to the XFT file node.

I have already explored standard document-related classes and function modules, but so far I haven't identified the correct XFT API.

Questions:

  • Is there a standard class or function module for uploading content into an XFT file node?
  • Is there any recommended approach or framework for this scenario?
  • Has anyone implemented something similar in ECC or S/4HANA?

Any guidance, examples, or pointers would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere: Extract, Transform & Load (ETL) - Best Practices
entry.content:
@MustafaBensan - Please find below response.

1. SAP BDC connect is primarily for data federation scenario (its not ETL), enables bidirectional, zero-copy/delta-share data sharing between SAP BDC and external platforms (Enterprise databricks, Snowflake, GCP etc).

2 and 3  Replication flow is recommended approach to replicate SAP-Managed or Customer-Managed Data Products into target such as GCP.

4. Refer Blog series: SAP Business Data Cloud Series – Part 1: Introduction to Data Products and Part 3.

Happy learning,

Jeet

entry.title: Re: Skipping Invoice Checks for Reversal Events in MRM_CH...
entry.content:
Deart @kienngo,

In MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD, reversal is not provided as a separate ACTION value. SAP provides actions such as Check, Post, Park etc. but not a dedicated Reverse action. Therefore reversal must be excluded based on the reversal fields available in HEADERDATA

The implemented logic now runs the validation only for normal Check and Post actions and skips the validation when HEADERDATA-REVERSEDOCUMENT or HEADERDATA-REVERSEDOCUMENTFISCALYEAR is filled. This allows supplier invoice reversal to proceed, while the check remains active for normal invoice checking and posting. I verified the case in my environment, during the check or post test I received validation

but when I click Reverse the system did not validation

"------------------------------------------------------------

" MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD

" Validate normal Check/Post, but skip reversal

"------------------------------------------------------------

IF ( action = if_ex_mrm_check_invoice_cloud=>c_action_check

     OR action = if_ex_mrm_check_invoice_cloud=>c_action_post )

   AND headerdata-reversedocument IS INITIAL

   AND headerdata-reversedocumentfiscalyear IS INITIAL.

  APPEND VALUE #(

    messagetype      = if_ex_mrm_check_invoice_cloud=>c_messagetype_error

    messageid        = 'MRM_BADI_CLOUD'

    messagenumber    = '001'

    messagevariable1 = 'Posting/checking is not allowed.'

  ) TO messages.

ENDIF.

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: SAP EAM Data Profiling in Utilities: What are your proven technical and business rules?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I'm reaching out to gather insights on understanding what are data profiling best practices specifically within the utilities industry. I'm particularly interested in understanding both technical implementation approaches and business rule perspectives that has worked, I understand this is very specific topic but I'm looking for starting discussion on proven and experience folks who are working or worked in EAM space.

What I'm looking for:

Technical Perspective:

  • What were the first 5–10 proven EAM data quality rules (Completeness, Accuracy, Timeliness & Consistency) you would implement and which would you explicitly defer?
  • What are critical objects in SAP EAM (Equipment, Functional Location, Task list, Maintenance Plan)? 
  • At what point does transactional data profiling become valuable for you?
  • How do you balance strict MDG enforcement (mandatory/blocked fields) vs scored or monitored rules in utilities EAM?
  • Which SAP aligned tools have you found most effective for EAM data profiling and governance and why?

Business Perspective:

  • In SAP S/4HANA Utilities EAM, where have you seen data profiling create the most measurable value? (e.g., maintenance planning reliability, regulatory readiness, asset health or reporting accuracy)
  • How did you determine the right scope for data profiling without over‑engineering it during ECC → S/4HANA transformations? Regulatory compliance considerations affecting data quality rules
  • Which regulatory or compliance drivers (PHMSA, NERC, FERC, safety‑critical assets) directly shaped your data profiling rules—and how do you keep them auditable long‑term?
  • How are you implementing asset‑class‑specific business rules in S/4HANA
    (e.g., valves, mains, transformers) without creating performance or rule‑maintenance issues?

I'd appreciate any experiences, lessons learned, or resources you can share!

Thanks in advance for your insights.

entry.title: Re: Problem reinstalling SAP ASE
entry.content:
@oneeyeman1

Your latest comment and attachments point at a smattering of issues ...

  1. The image with the message "The product configuration failed." is a generic message that states something went wrong during the installation; details will have been supplied in other windows/messages and/or in the various installation logs. For the most part this image doesn't tell us anything useful.
  2. The image with the message "The Replication Management Agent failed to start." would seem to indicate that you told the installation process to a) install the HADR product and b) create (at least some of) the HADR components (this would include the Replication Management Agent - RMA); if you really want to know what the issue was with the RMA creation you would need to review the log file(s) mentioned in the message. Having said that ...
  3. In your comment you mention "The error(s) is about SAP HADR.  /  What is this piece?".  From this it sounds like you don't know what HADR is and therefore you probably don't want to install HADR; however, from the bits-n-pieces of details provided it would appear you did ask the installer to not only install the HADR software but to also create (at least some of) the HADR components.
  4. You attempted to manually startup the ASE instance (startserver -f RUN_REINCLOUD) but that failed with the message "kernel basis_dlock: file '/opt/sap/data/master.dat' already in use by an ASE"; as the message states, the ASE instance was already up and running; at that point had you run a 'ps -aef' you should have seen  a process running the /opt/sap/ASE-16_1/bin/dataserver binary; by itself this error message is not an issue ... it's merely telling you the ASE instance is already up and running

-----------------------------

We're not told the series of steps, options and arguments you provided to the installer so I'm going to assume you did something like the following:

1) When asked to choose from the following ASE installation steps:

  • Typical (this is referring to a typical ASE installation and consists of the basics you'll need to get an ASE instance up and running)
  • Typical SAP  ASE, database always-on option (as stated in small print: this is ASE *plus* the High Availability / Disaster Recovery - HADR option; fwiw, 'always-on' == 'HADR'; this is an advanced option that should only be chosen if you are looking to setup a HADR environment)
  • Full (this is referring to all ASE components; does not include always-on==HADR)
  • Custom (same as Full but you get to pick-n-choose which components to install)

From what you've posted it appears you chose to install the Typical SAP  ASE, database always-on option.  (Since you don't know what HADR is, and assuming you just want the barebones basic ASE software installed, you should have chosen the Typical option.)

2) After the software (ASE and HADR in this case) is installed the installer will ask if you want the installer to create some instances of various components (eg, ASE dataserver, backupserver , etc).  I'm guessing you requested to create a basic HADR environment; during the creation of the HADR components you received the error message (attached image) stating there was a problem starting the RMA).  (Again, choosing the Typical option should not install the HADR components, which in turn means the RMA would not be installed/configured, which in turn would eliminate the RMA error message).

3) I'm guessing you also asked the installer to create an ASE instance (named REINCLOUD); since you don't mention any other installer-related error messages I'm going to assume this ASE instance was in fact a) created and b) successfully started since ...

4) After the installer finished (albeit with errors) you chose to manually start the ASE instance (startserver -f RUN_REINCLOUD), but because the installer has already started this ASE instance you were provided with the errorlog message "'/opt/sap/data/master.dat' already in use by an ASE".

-----------------------------

Suggested next steps ...

  1. Make sure all sybase processes are shutdown and/or killed (this includes dataserver and backupserver binaries as well as any HADR-related binaries; if in doubt run 'ps -aef' and look for anything running under the /opt/sap directory, then kill any such processes
  2. Delete the entire directory /opt/sap; you want to start over from scratch with no lingering issues from the previous/failed installation(s).
  3. Run the installer and when it asks you to Choose Install Set, chose the first option - Typical; again, I'm assuming you only want to install the base minimum software to get an ASE instance up and running
  4. After the installer exits, and assuming no errors/issues, and assuming you requested a sample ASE instance to be created, the ASE instance should already be up and running; if the ASE instance is not running for some reason then go ahead and manually start the ASE instance (startserver -f RUN_...)

entry.title: Missing Association After Data Product Deployment in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

A Sales table is modeled as a fact view with an association to a Product dimension (modeled as a separate graphical view). Both views are exposed for consumption. A data product is created on top of these two artifacts - Sales and Product and deployed. And the data product is installed in the target space while both views are available in the target space, the association is missing. Is this by design.

Could you help clarify why this happens. Thanks!
--

entry.title: Re: Ask Me Anything with SAP Champions – Monthly Series (Session 3)
entry.content:
Hola Sandra,
Antes que nada, quiero agradecerte por tu esfuerzo y por compartir tu conocimiento. Tu canal me ha sido de mucha ayuda para comprender mejor varios conceptos de ABAP en ECC y, posteriormente, al migrar a S/4HANA, entenderlos con mayor profundidad.

Quisiera aprovechar para plantearte algunas consultas desde la experiencia práctica:

  1. Actualmente utilizo Eclipse con ADT para crear CDS Views.
    ¿Cuál es la diferencia entre exponer un OData/API utilizando Service Definition y Service Binding frente al enfoque tradicional mediante /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE?
    En la práctica he notado que, al usar Service Binding, se habilita automáticamente el paginado, lo cual resulta bastante conveniente.

  2. Para trabajar con Fiori, ¿qué roles o permisos básicos debería tener un desarrollador o usuario funcional para desarrollar y probar aplicaciones correctamente?

  3. En escenarios donde ya existen transacciones ERP (SAP GUI):

    • ¿Cómo se pueden reutilizar esas transacciones para llevarlas a Fiori?
    • ¿Qué sucede con el concepto de desarrollos Z en este contexto?

  4. He tenido reuniones con varios proveedores SAP y frecuentemente mencionan herramientas como SAP Build (low-code / drag & drop), Joule, CAP con JavaScript, etc.
    Suelen comentar que la curva de aprendizaje de ABAP es compleja y que las nuevas generaciones no muestran tanto interés en aprenderlo.
    Mi duda es: ¿existe alguna señal clara de que SAP planee eliminar ABAP en el futuro, o más bien sigue siendo un lenguaje estratégico?

  5. Desde tu experiencia, ¿qué sugerencias darías para que los desarrolladores Fiori y los desarrolladores ABAP puedan coordinarse mejor y lograr entregas exitosas y sostenibles?

  6. Utilizo AMDP y Table Functions, y he notado que los AMDP ofrecen muy buen rendimiento cuando se usan desde transacciones ERP.
    En cambio, las Table Functions las he usado principalmente para crear APIs.
    ¿Es esta una buena práctica o están pensadas para otros escenarios específicos?

  7. Actualmente trabajo mucho con RAP, y en algunos casos me resulta más sencillo utilizar AMDP con SQLScript para evitar demasiada lógica en las transacciones.
    ¿Qué recomendaciones darías para que este tipo de soluciones sean escalables y mantenibles?

  8. En cuanto a Clean Code:

    • Si ya existen muchas transacciones Z y además, a nivel RISE, se han implementado CDS,
    • ¿cómo recomiendas identificar code smells en un ERP amplio?
    • ¿Qué pasos dar para mejorar progresivamente la calidad del código?

  9. No he utilizado mucho el IDE de BTP, ya que prefiero Eclipse, pero noto que BTP está más orientado al frontend.
    ¿Cuál es la diferencia entre desarrollar una aplicación Fiori directamente en el ERP frente a una aplicación CAP en BTP?
    ¿Dónde se almacena el código en cada caso y cómo conviven con los paquetes del ERP?

  10. Finalmente, ¿cuál consideras que es la mejor forma de introducirse en el mundo CAP?

    • ¿Cuándo recomiendas usarlo vs FIORI?
    • ¿Cuáles serían las mejores prácticas para integrarlo correctamente con CDS y RAP?

entry.title: What’s New in SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone: 1H 2026 Release Highlights
entry.content:
The 1H 2026 release of SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone brings a focused set of enhancements designed to reduce friction, eliminate context-switching, and create a more cohesive employee experience. Whether employees are navigating a life event, accessing HR analytics, or completing assigned learning, Work Zone continues to evolve as the single hub where work gets done.

WHAT’S NEW

Highlights from the 1H 2026 release

01   GUIDED EXPERIENCE

Marital status change — a streamlined life-event experience

Life events often trigger a cascade of HR updates across systems — benefits enrollment, personal information, emergency contacts, and dependent changes. The new guided experience for marital status changes breaks this complex process into clear, interactive steps, weaving together data from both SAP and non-SAP systems in a single flow. Progress is saved automatically, so employees can pause and pick up right where they left off without losing any information.

✓ Simplifies life-event processing, reduces errors, and ensures every related update is completed in one streamlined experience — no steps missed, no systems overlooked.

02   ANALYTICS

SAP SuccessFactors story reports — now embedded in Work Zone

Employees and managers can now access SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) story reports directly within Work Zone, without navigating away to a separate reporting environment. Analytics surface where and when they’re relevant, inside the flow of work — not as a separate destination requiring a context switch.

✓  Reinforces Work Zone as a single hub for productivity and insights, making data-driven decisions faster and more intuitive for every employee.

03   LEARNING

Learning Plan cards — refreshed for visual consistency

Learning Plan cards in Work Zone have been refreshed to align with the updated learning experience introduced on the SAP SuccessFactors Home Page. Both visual design and functional behavior are now consistent across environments, creating a unified learning experience regardless of where an employee starts their day.

✓  Improves usability, reduces confusion, and helps employees engage with learning activities more effectively across Work Zone and SuccessFactors.

JOINING US AT SAP SAPPHIRE, ORLANDO 2026?  See Work Zone in action.

Hear directly from Duval County Public Schools on how they’re unifying HR with SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone — and what that transformation looks like in practice for a large public sector organization.  

SESSION HCM1783 : Duval Schools is unifying HR with the SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone solution

Together, these three enhancements reduce friction, improve usability, and strengthen Work Zone as a unified hub for employee services, insights, and learning. The 1H 2026 release continues the journey toward a digital workplace that works the way employees do — not the other way around.

Explore the full release details in the SAP SuccessFactors What’s New Viewer, and don’t forget to add session HCM1783 to your SAP Sapphire agenda.

entry.title: SAP Sapphire Orlando 2026: What's new in SAP Business Data Cloud: AI innovations
entry.content:
My session 

“What's new in SAP Business Data Cloud: AI innovations | BDC2545” 

At Sapphire, I’ll walk through what’s new in SAP Business Data Cloud specifically for AI innovation. The theme is simple: data is the foundation of our AI-first and autonomous enterprise strategy. The session focuses on how we’re evolving BDC’s architecture—across the data fabric and the “knowledge core”—to better support real AI use cases. 

 We’ve made meaningful investments in data products, a strong semantic layer, and knowledge-graph capabilities—so that enterprise data isn’t just available, but understandable. That understanding is what enables trustworthy automation and agentic experiences without losing the business context that makes SAP data so valuable. 

Want to learn more? 

If you want to go deeper after the session, I’d suggest three practical next steps. 

  • Review our external-facing materials on BDC’s semantic context and how it fits into SAP’s autonomous enterprise strategy. 
  • Join enablement sessions that unpack the “knowledge core” concepts in more detail. 
  • Attend (or watch recordings of) related sessions on data products—that connects directly to making AI work reliably. 

Happy to hear your thoughts 

  • As we move toward agents, what is your biggest concern: the AI's ability to actually understand your specific business logic and constraints or will AI provide business contextual actions accurately?
  • When we talk about 'Revenue' or 'Customer' in your organization, does that mean the same thing in your data lake as it does in your ERP? Or is the 'business meaning' getting lost in translation? 

Looking for to seeing you in SAP Sapphire!

entry.title: PR/PO 审批流如何对接外部 BPM？S/4HANA Cloud 的边界与突破路径
entry.content:
背景

随着企业数字化转型的深入推进，越来越多的企业选择采用统一的外部 BPM（业务流程管理）平台，集中管理跨系统的审批工作流，以取代传统的线下纸质签核方式。在此背景下，如何将 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition（以下简称 S/4）的采购申请单（PR）与采购订单（PO）审批流程与外部 BPM 系统打通，成为许多企业在实施过程中面临的常见需求。

本文整理了针对此场景的官方立场、已知限制，以及一种可供参考的自定义集成思路。

标准产品的现状与限制

目前，S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 的 PR 与 PO API 不原生支持将系统内置工作流与外部 BPM 系统进行集成。类似需求曾多次通过 SAP  Customer Influence Portal (CIP) 提出，产品研发团队以以下理由明确回应，短期内不予支持：

  • "Violating design rules"（违反产品设计原则）
  • "Not planned within SAP's standard in the foreseeable future"（在可预见的未来不纳入标准规划）

相关 CIP 历史提案可参考：

  • https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/232148
  • https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/267559
  • https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/267560

值得关注的是，该需求于 2025 年被社区再次提出（https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/358215），目前因投票数尚未达到进入评审的门槛，暂未被 SAP 产品团队纳入 Review 阶段。如您认同此需求，欢迎前往为该提案投票，推动其进入正式评审流程。

SAP 的官方建议是优先使用 S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 内置的单据审批功能。如对该功能有改善建议，可继续通过 CIP 渠道提报。

值得注意的是，企业采购更专业的 BPM 平台集中管理数字单据工作流是业界常态。SAP 也对此类诉求表示理解，并建议通过自定义开发的方式作为折中方案。

现有折中方案的局限

在社区讨论与客户实践中，常见的两种折中方案均存在明显的限制：

方案一：外部 BPM 审批前置
在 S/4 中创建 PR 或 PO 之前，先在外部系统完成审批，审批通过后再通过 API 创建单据。

  • 问题：MRP 运算会自动在系统内生成 PR，与"先审批后建单"的逻辑产生结构性冲突，无法完全适用。

方案二：自定义字段管控状态
通过自定义字段存储外部审批状态，并利用 BAdI 判断是否触发系统内审批流。

  • 问题：仅适用于 PO，无法直接覆盖 PR 的审批场景。

建议的自定义集成思路

针对上述限制，以下提供一种更完整的自定义集成思路，供技术团队参考验证。具体可行性须以实际测试结果为准。

核心设计原则

由于 MRP 会自动产生 PR，建议不阻止 PR 的创建，改为通过控制 PR 转 PO 的转换来实现实质上的采购申请审批管控。

3.1 PR 管控方案

步骤一：为 PR 行项目新增自定义字段

在业务场景 Procurement: Purchase Requisition Item（MM_PURREQN_ITEM）中，通过 App Extensibility 新增一个自定义字段，用于存储外部 BPM 系统回传的审批状态（例如：待审批 / 已通过 / 已拒绝）。

参考文档：App Extensibility: Assign and Process Purchase Requisitions

步骤二：MRP 产生 PR 时初始化自定义字段

通过 BAdI PPH_MRP_PURREQ_CFL，在 MRP 自动生成 PR 的同时，对上述自定义字段进行初始化赋值（默认为"待审批"状态），确保所有自动生成的 PR 均进入外部审批流。

参考文档：Purchase Requisition Enhancements for MRP

步骤三：外部 BPM 回写审批结果

外部 BPM 系统完成审批后，通过 Purchase Requisition API（CE_PURCHASEREQUISITION_0001）将审批结果回写至 S/4 的自定义字段，更新对应 PR 的审批状态。

参考文档：Purchase Requisition OData API (CE_PURCHASEREQUISITION_0001)

步骤四：PR 转 PO 时进行状态拦截

在 PR 转 PO 的转换阶段，通过 BAdI BD_MMPUR_FINAL_CHECK_PO 检查上述自定义字段的值，仅允许审批状态为"已通过"的 PR 生成对应的 PO，从而在系统层面完成审批管控。

参考文档：Check of Purchase Order Before Saving

3.2 PO 本身的审批管控

如需对 PO 本身（而非 PR）进行外部审批管控，同样可参考上述步骤四的 BAdI BD_MMPUR_FINAL_CHECK_PO 进行扩展实现，通过自定义字段存储外部审批结果，并在 PO 保存前进行状态校验。

整体架构示意

S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
     │
     ├─ MRP 自动产生 PR
     │    └─ BAdI PPH_MRP_PURREQ_CFL：初始化自定义字段 → "待审批"
     │
     ├─ 手动创建 PR
     │    └─ 自定义字段默认值："待审批"
     │
     ├─ 推送审批请求至外部 BPM 系统
     │
     ├─ 外部 BPM 完成审批，通过 CE_PURCHASEREQUISITION_0001 API 回写结果
     │    └─ 自定义字段更新 → "已通过 / 已拒绝"
     │
     └─ PR → PO 转换
          └─ BAdI BD_MMPUR_FINAL_CHECK_PO：校验自定义字段
               ├─ 已通过 → 允许生成 PO
               └─ 待审批 / 已拒绝 → 阻止生成 PO

注意事项

  1. 此方案为自定义开发思路，需经过完整的技术验证，不构成 SAP 的官方支持承诺。
  2. MRP 场景下自定义字段的初始化逻辑需仔细设计，避免影响现有 MRP 运行性能。
  3. 外部 BPM 与 S/4 之间的 API 集成需考虑认证、错误处理与重试机制。
  4. 若对 S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 的采购审批功能有具体改善建议，建议继续通过 SAP Customer Influence Portal 提出，推动产品标准化支持。

小结

S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 目前在标准产品层面不支持 PR/PO 与外部 BPM 的工作流集成，但通过合理的自定义开发——利用 App Extensibility 新增自定义字段、搭配 BAdI 增强与 OData API——可以在不干扰 MRP 自动化逻辑的前提下，实现对 PR 转 PO 节点的审批管控，为企业的统一审批平台集成提供一条可行的技术路径。

希望本文对有类似需求的技术团队有所帮助。如有实践经验或更好的方案，欢迎在评论区分享讨论。

entry.title: BTP Trial staging cell blocking Docker Hub and npm registry - n8n deployment
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am trying to deploy n8n (a workflow automation tool) on SAP BTP Trial
using Cloud Foundry, but the staging cells are blocking all outbound
network connections. I have tried two approaches and both fail.

ENVIRONMENT:
- BTP Trial Org: e10d2d25trial
- Space: dev
- Region: us10-001
- CF API: https://api.cf.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com

APPROACH 1: Docker deployment
Manifest:
docker:
image: n8nio/n8n:latest

Error:
Failed getting docker image manifest by tag: pinging container registry
registry-1.docker.io: Get "https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/": dial tcp
44.208.62.237:443: connect: connection refused

APPROACH 2: nodejs_buildpack deployment (with vendored node_modules)
Error:
npm error code ENOTCACHED
npm error request to https://registry.npmjs.org/n8n failed:
cache mode is 'only-if-cached' but no cached response is available.

WHAT I HAVE TRIED:
1. Vendoring node_modules locally and pushing with buildpack
2. Excluding package-lock.json from upload via .cfignore
3. Setting NODE_MODULES_CACHE=false in manifest env vars
4. Using cf push --docker-image flag instead of manifest
5. Clean reinstall of node_modules locally before pushing

QUESTIONS:
1. Does SAP BTP Trial block outbound connections from staging cells
to Docker Hub (registry-1.docker.io)?
2. Does SAP BTP Trial block outbound connections to npmjs.org
from staging cells?
3. Is there a supported way to deploy Docker images on BTP Trial?
4. Is there an SAP Container Registry I can use to host the image
that is accessible from BTP Trial staging cells?
5. Is there any workaround to deploy a Node.js app with vendored
dependencies without the buildpack trying to reach the internet?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: modify designation
entry.content:
That error message is actually pointing to a missing material description in French. In SAP S/4HANA, every material must have a description maintained in the logon language you are working in. Based on your error message, I believe your system session is in French and hence, the system expects the material to have a French description. If it does not, you will see the “No designation in FR. Complete the article.” error when trying to process dependent documents like a Purchase Requisition.

Why you can’t change it in Basic Data View 1 is because, the material description is language-dependent. In Basic Data View 1, you only see the description in the language it was originally created. If the French description wasn’t maintained at material master creation, you can’t edit it directly there.

Perform the below steps to fix this

  • Go to transaction MM02 (Change Material).
  • Enter the material number.
  • In the Basic Data 1 view, switch your logon language to French (or maintain the description in the language field).
  • Enter the French description for the material.
  • Save the material.

Now, when you go back to your Purchase Requisition, the system will recognize that the material has a valid description in French, and you’ll be able to modify the quantity without the error.

entry.title: Re: Freight Unit with Non Stock Material
entry.content:
Good day @mafusco_034 

Thank you for your question.

For a non-stock material, you can only create freight unit if the item creates a transportation-relevant requirement through a supported base process. Reason is, a freight unit represents a transportation requirement. In the standard process, you create freight unit from supported predecessor documents such as sales orders, purchase orders, stock transport orders, or other supported logistics processes. If your non-stock is only an expense, service, or account-assigned item, then this does not normally represent what physical cargo for transportation planning is designed for.

Here is what i recommend you should do:

  1. First, confirm whether your business item is a physical product that requires transportation.
  2. Check whether your defined process is one of the supported TM base processes, for example Sell from Stock BD9, Direct Procurement with Inbound Delivery 2TX, or Stock Transfer with Delivery BME.
  3. Test the same process once with the non-stock material and once with a stock or logistics-relevant material.
  4. Check your sales order or purchase order document flow.
  5. Use Manage Freight Units to search for the result.                                                                                                                                              
  6. If your stock material creates a freight unit and the non-stock material does not, then that is a behaviour relating to the non-stock item not creating a transportation-relevant requirement.
  7. If your business requires transportation planning for a physical item, consider changing the product and process design to a logistics-relevant process. The private cloud or on-premise offers some enhancement options, but the Public Cloud depends on released configuration and extensibility only.

Here are references for more guidance

3343471 - Freight Unit is not created for non-stock material - SAP for Me

2661319 - Freight Unit not automatically created with SAP TM 9.5 and SAP TM embedded in SAP S/4HANA - SAP for Me

Creating Freight Units, Configuring Transportation Management, Managing Freight Units from Different Base Processes

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: [i] CHECKING FOR ADDITIONAL PARAMETERS IN CREATE DUNN...
entry.content:
Hello @johnangelo_buena 

Thank you for your question.

No, you cannot use standard configuration or key user extensibility to add LoB as an additional parameter directly in the Create Dunning Notices app. The reason is, the app is a SAP GUI for HTML transaction, and the standard selection screen is not extended like a fully extensible SAPUI5 app. Therefore, you cannot add a new custom selection parameter directly to the dunning run screen.

The possibility you have is as follows:

  1. You can make use of the existing standard dunning parameters and account restrictions.
  2. Check whether Dunning Area can represent your business requirement, but only if it is functionally correct.
  3. Use My Dunning Proposals to review the proposal before you send the notices.                                          
  4. If your requirement is only to show LoB on the dunning notice, check dunning notice custom field and form extensibility.
  5. However, if your requirement is mandatory as a new dunning run selection criterion, I suggest you raise a feature request through the Influence Opportunity Homepage - Customer Influence portal.

Here are references for more guidance

3237273 - Create dunning notices per dunning level in S/4HANA Cloud public edition - SAP for Me

3050562 - Document & has payment method for incoming payments, no dunning notice - SAP for Me

3612451 - Custom Fields - Frequently Asked Questions - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

3338192 - Dunning Frequency Error When Creating Dunning Notices - SAP for Me

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Grouping WBS issue during MRP Run
entry.content:
Why it happens is because, in Grouping WBS scenarios, SAP allows you to collect procurement requirements at the grouping WBS for external procurement.

However, planned orders for in-house production are also defaulting to the grouping WBS because the system is treating the grouping WBS as the “account assignment receiver” for all requirements under that hierarchy.

The lower-level WBS elements are linked to networks/activities, but unless you explicitly tell the system to pass the account assignment down, MRP will assign the grouping WBS.

To ensure planned orders use lower-level WBS, perform the below steps

1) Check the Account Assignment Settings in the Network Activities
In each activity, ensure the account assignment is set to the respective lower-level WBS (WBS1, WBS2, WBS3). If the activity is pointing to the grouping WBS, MRP will follow that.

2) Review the “Grouping WBS” Customizing
In transaction OPSN (Project Definition → Project Settings for MRP), check how grouping WBS is defined. There is a setting for whether requirements are collected at the grouping WBS or passed down to lower-level WBS. For your case, you want external procurement → grouping WBS, but in-house production → lower-level WBS. That requires differentiating the assignment rules.

3) MRP Group / MRP Controller Settings
Assign different MRP Groups to the materials depending on procurement type. For in-house production materials, configure the MRP Group so that the system uses the network/WBS element account assignment. For externally procured materials, configure the MRP Group to collect requirements at the grouping WBS.

4) Check Parameter Effectivity (Model Unit)
Since you are using parameter effectivity, ensure the link between model unit values and WBS elements is consistent. If the system does not recognize the lower-level WBS as the receiver, it will default back to the grouping WBS.

5) Component Issue (Movement 261)
Once planned orders are correctly assigned to lower-level WBS, production orders created from them will also carry the correct WBS. This ensures that goods issue (261) will consume from the grouping WBS stock but settle against the lower-level WBS order without error.

entry.title: Re: Bring your own Git for SAP BTP ABAP Environment
entry.content:
@Atul_Joshi85 

Thanks for your interesting questions.

1. See Information for Audit | SAP Help Portal on how git-based lifecycle influences auditability. In short, it is improved because git commits build up on each other like a block chain.

2. Fingerprints are a means to ensure git-based transports can only imported in the same landscape where they were developed and tested. If you need to transfer developments to a different account's landscape, you should export/import them with abapGit, see Working with abapGit | SAP Help Portal

3. I do not see a direct relation between the choice of external Git providers on the energy transition aspect in the utility sector.

Hope to help,

Christoph

entry.title: Re: Maintenance Order Actual and Planned Costs fields not...
entry.content:
Good day @elvagha 

Thank you for your question.

I would not use I_MaintenanceOrderBasic as the primary data source for planned and actual cost reporting. It is mainly suitable for maintenance order basic/header information, so fields such as TotalPlannedCost and TotalActualCost may not be available there.

I recommend to first check C_MaintOrdActlPlndCostQuery, because it is used for maintenance order planned and actual cost analysis. For detailed actual cost reporting, also check I_MaintOderActualCostDataCube and C_MaintOrdActualCostDataQ.

As a next step, consider

  1. Opening View Browser, search for C_MaintOrdActlPlndCostQuery and confirm that the planned and actual cost measures are available.                          
  2. Seach for I_MaintOderActualCostDataCube if you require detailed actual cost reporting.   
  3. Build or adapt the analytical query/reporting object based on the released data source available in your tenant.
  4. Use Custom Communication Scenarios and Communication Arrangement to expose the reporting object via OData.      
  5. Validate the values against the Maintenance Order Costs app before consuming the data in Power BI.   

     

Here are references for more guidance

3024517 - I_MaintOrderActualCostDataCube and C_MaintOrdActualCostDataQ do not contain any field key description - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

3700830 - Real-time data not updating in Maintenance Order Costs - Fiori App Id: F4603 - SAP for Me

3283767 - Costs don't appear in the app 'Maintenance Order Costs' F4603 - SAP for Me

Working with Technical Aspects

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Introducing the new SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library
entry.content:
Few reasons why we added the login behavior :

  • Login and registration aligns with SAP support services such as SAP for Me, allowing you to use a single set of credentials across platforms.
  • Registered users gain access to exclusive resources and information that are available only after login.
  • Your settings, selections, and preferences are saved and carried across sessions for a consistent, tailored experience.

entry.title: Fullstack CAP Application with HANA Cloud (Decoupled Architecture) [Part-3]
entry.content:
Setting Up SAP HANA Cloud on BTP

In Part 2 , we added CDS Annotations and previewed the Fiori UI locally with SQLite. Now it is time to move to the cloud database layer.

In this Part, we will set up SAP HANA Cloud on SAP BTP so our CAP application can use it as the production database. This involves several one-time BTP configuration steps before we can deploy anything.

Here is what we will do in this part:

1. Verify SAP HANA Cloud is entitled at the Global Account level
2. Assign the HANA Cloud service plans to your Subaccount
3. Subscribe to HANA Cloud Tools via the Service Marketplace
4. Assign the required HANA Cloud roles to your user
5. Create a SAP HANA Cloud database instance
6. Map the Cloud Foundry environment to the HANA Cloud instance

All of these steps are done in the SAP BTP Cockpit and SAP HANA Cloud Central.

STEP 1 — VERIFY HANA CLOUD ENTITLEMENTS IN THE GLOBAL ACCOUNT

Before creating any HANA Cloud resources, confirm that your Global Account has the SAP HANA Cloud service entitlement available.

In the SAP BTP Cockpit, navigate to:

Global Account → Entitlements → Service Assignments

In the search box, type "HANA" to filter the services.

 You should see the services available.

 

If you see these listed, your Global Account is entitled.

Proceed to Step 2.

NOTE: If SAP HANA Cloud does not appear here, contact your BTP Global Account administrator to enable the entitlement.


STEP 2 — ASSIGN HANA CLOUD SERVICE PLANS TO YOUR SUBACCOUNT

Entitlements available at the Global Account level must be explicitly assigned to a Subaccount before they can be used there.

Navigate to:

Global Account → Entitlements → Entity Assignments

Filter by your subaccount (e.g., "trial") and locate the SAP HANA Cloud service. Click "Edit" and add the required service plans to your subaccount.

- Search for "hana" in the services list on the left
- Select "SAP HANA Cloud" from the results
- On the right panel, check the following plans and Click "Add Service Plans" to confirm

 

After saving, the Entity Assignments page will show SAP HANA Cloud with the assigned plans for your subaccount.

 

STEP 3 — SUBSCRIBE TO HANA CLOUD TOOLS IN THE SERVICE MARKETPLACE

SAP HANA Cloud is managed through a browser-based graphical tool called SAP HANA Cloud Central. To access it, you must first subscribe to the "hana-cloud-tools" application in your subaccount's Service Marketplace.

Navigate to your Subaccount, then go to: Services → Service Marketplace

Search for "HANA CLOUD". Click on the "SAP HANA Cloud" tile.

 

 

In the right panel, click "Create". A wizard dialog will open.

 

 Click "Create". This creates a subscription to HANA Cloud Central.

 

Navigate to:  Services → Instances and Subscriptions

You should now see "SAP HANA Cloud" listed under Subscriptions with Status: "Subscribed".

 

STEP 4 — ASSIGN HANA CLOUD ROLES TO YOUR USER

Before you can open HANA Cloud Central, your BTP user needs the correct role collections assigned. Without them, you will see a "Not authorized" error when trying to access HANA Cloud Central.

 

 

 

The required SAP HANA Cloud role collections are:
https://help.sap.com/docs/hana-cloud/sap-hana-cloud-administration-guide/role-collections-for-sap-hana-cloud

 

To assign these roles, navigate to your Subaccount:

 

Security → Users

Click on your user  to open the details panel.
Click "Assign Role Collection".

 

 

In the "Assign Role Collection" dialog, search for "SAP HANA CLOUD".
Select all the required role collections:

 

Click "Assign Role Collection" to save.

 

STEP 5 — CREATE THE SAP HANA CLOUD DATABASE INSTANCE

Now open SAP HANA Cloud Central. You can access it from:

Services → Instances and Subscriptions → SAP HANA Cloud → "Go to Application"

HANA Cloud Central will open in a new browser tab. The Instances page will show "No instances" — we need to create one.

 

Click "Create Instance". A multi-step wizard opens with 8 steps.

1

 

2

 

3

 

4

 

5

 

6

 

7

 

8

 

After creation, HANA Cloud Central will show both instances as "Running":

 

STEP 6 — MAP THE CLOUD FOUNDRY ENVIRONMENT TO THE HANA CLOUD INSTANCE

The HANA Cloud instance was created but it needs to be mapped to your Cloud Foundry organization and space. This mapping is what enables CAP to create HDI (HANA Deployment Infrastructure) containers for schema deployment when you run cds deploy --to hana.

Click on the "SHC_DB" instance to open its details. 

 

Click "Manage Configuration" → go to the "Instance Mapping" tab.

Click "Add Mapping". You will be redirected to sign into the CF environment.

 

Sign in with your default identity provider credentials. After authentication, return to the Instance Mapping tab and click "Add Mapping" again.

 

Now a new row appears. Fill in the mapping:

Click "Review and Save" to confirm the mapping. The HANA Cloud instance is now connected to your Cloud Foundry space and ready to receive HDI container deployments from the CAP application.

 


SUMMARY

In Part 3, we successfully:

✔ Verified SAP HANA Cloud entitlements at the Global Account level
✔ Assigned HANA Cloud service plans to the trial subaccount
✔ Subscribed to SAP HANA Cloud Tools (hana-cloud-tools) via Service Marketplace
✔ Assigned all required SAP HANA Cloud role collections to our user
✔ Created a SAP HANA Cloud database instance (SHC_DB) with a linked Data Lake (SHC_DL) using the free tier
✔ Mapped the Cloud Foundry organization and space to the HANA instance to enable HDI container creation

The SAP HANA Cloud infrastructure is now fully set up and ready. In Part 4, we will connect the CAP application to this HANA Cloud instance and deploy the backend to Cloud Foundry.

entry.title: How to Set Up a Python Virtual Environment
entry.content:
Introduction:

A Python virtual environment is a local directory that maintains project dependencies distinct from the rest of the system. It prevents conflicts of version and provides a uniform development in various presuppositions. This is needed to create clean Python applications that are maintainable and scalable.

 

Open Visual studio and create a folder { To learn on How to step up Visual Studio for python programming , please refer my blog https://community.sap.com/t5/artificial-intelligence-blogs-posts/ai-steps-to-setup-python-programming/ba-p/14381631 }

 

 

Create Virtual environment using python -m venv {environment name}

 

 

 

Create folder for database , ui, and text file for requirements/dependencies

 

 

Add dependencies in requirement file

 

 

Install dependencies using command  

pip install -r {requirement text file name}

 

 

 

Activate Virtual environment

 

 

 

Summary 

A python virtual environment helps to isolate project dependencies and prevents conflicts with the rest of the system. It provides uniform, hygienic and controllable growth in various setups.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Q1 2026 Release: Insights from Hands‑On Experience
entry.content:
Hello Community,

It’s been a while since I last wrote an SAP blog. Over the past couple of years, I had the opportunity to work with several analytics tools and SAP Analytics Cloud has consistently stood out as a unique and fast evolving member of the analytics family. One thing that becomes very clear when you work with SAC is how fast it evolves. Just when you start getting comfortable with the current features, SAP introduces new updates that make the tool even more powerful and refined. This constant pace of innovation is something I genuinely enjoy, even though it can be challenging at times.

With every release, there is always something new to explore, test and sometimes even relearn. In this blog, I would like to share few features from the Q1 2026 SAC release that I personally encountered and found especially useful during my day‑to‑day work.

1. Story Versioning

Story Versioning is a great addition that really stands out in this release. It introduces a framework that allows dashboard designers to create, manage and restore up to 10 versions of a story, making experimentation much safer.

In real projects, it’s common to try different layouts, KPIs or visual approaches. Especially after feedback from business users. Earlier, this often meant copying stories or manually tracking changes. With Story Versioning, you can confidently test ideas knowing that reverting to a previous version is just a few clicks away.

To create a version, open the story in Edit mode, navigate to the Save tab and choose Save as New Version. Versions can be accessed either directly from Edit mode via File Versions or from Files > My Stories > File Versions, where you can open or delete older versions. Each version also shows who created it, when it was last modified, and what changed. As, this is very helpful in team‑based environments.

2: Live Snowflake Connectivity

One of the bigger technical enhancements in this release is live connectivity to Snowflake, now supported even for planning models. This removes the need for data replication and allows SAC to work directly on real‑time Snowflake data.

Setting up the connection is straightforward. You create a new live connection from the Connections area, select Snowflake and provide the required details such as account, database, username, and private key. Once connected, you can create a live model in the Modeler and select the required Snowflake tables.

From my experience, this is particularly useful when data freshness is critical or when data volumes are large and duplication is not ideal.

3: Presentation Mode

Presentation Mode replaces the old full screen experience and brings Digital Boardroom like functionality directly into standard SAC stories, without requiring a separate license.

This is a minor but impactful change. Instead of switching tools or modes for executive presentations, you can now stay within the same story and still deliver a structured, professional presentation experience. It feels much more polished during live discussions and review meetings.

4: Ability to Import Pages

This is a simple feature, but one that saves time in real projects. You can now import pages from other stories into your current story.

In Edit mode, selecting Add Page > Import Story Pages allows you to browse existing stories and reuse entire pages. For large recurring reporting cycles, this avoids rebuilding content from scratch and helps maintain consistency across stories.

5: Complex Presentation Structure

With this update, SAC allows you to build nested presentation structures using topics and subtopics. Think of it as creating a logical tree. This is very useful when you have multiple sections like Overview, Detailed Analysis and Deep Dives.

You can configure this in Edit mode > File > Edit Story > Presentation Settings or from the Outline panel. Pages can be organized under parent topics and subtopics making navigation during presentations far more intuitive. This has been especially helpful in long management review sessions.

6: Flexible Control Panel in Present Mode

Present Mode also brings better control over navigation and layout. You can configure the top toolbar to be floating or fixed, set it to auto‑hide and work with a consistent vertical filter panel.

To configure this, go to Edit mode > File > Edit > View Time Settings, adjust the toolbar behavior and save the story. Small adjustments, but they make a noticeable difference during live presentations where screen space and clarity matter.

7: Replace Model Within Stories (My Personal Favorite)

This is easily one of my favorite updates in this release.

In the past, I have faced multiple situations where the data model needed to change midway through development or sometimes even much later. Anyone who has been there knows what that usually meant: rebuilding widgets, re‑binding measures, rechecking filters and losing a lot of time redoing work that was already finalized.

With the Replace Model feature, SAC has addressed a very real pain point. You can now replace an existing model with another model that has a similar structure, while preserving story layouts, widgets, and bindings. This has already saved a significant amount of time for many of us, especially in agile or evolving projects.

That said, I do feel this is a feature that should be used with awareness. Before replacing a model, it’s important to understand all dependencies like dimensions, measures, calculations and filters. SAC does a good job of automatically mapping objects based on names and highlights any mismatches but taking time to review warnings ensures nothing fails unexpectedly.

I think when used carefully, this feature is a huge productivity booster.

 

That being said, I am excited to keep learning and exploring new features as SAP Analytics Cloud continues to evolve. I would also love to hear from you and please feel free to share your experiences, learnings and the features that caught your attention in this or recent SAC releases.😊

entry.title: Strategic Change and SAP SuccessFactors: Why Senior HR Leaders Choose Max Success Plans
entry.content:
When we speak to Senior HR leaders they often say that they are being asked to do something fundamentally difficult: drive continuous organisational change while the ground beneath the organisation keeps shifting. They’re expected to modernise HR operations, harmonise workforce data, introduce AI responsibly, and enable faster workforce decisions - all without destabilising day‑to‑day operations or eroding trust.

The challenge isn’t recognising that transformation is ongoing. The harder question is how to structure the organisation around that reality so the HR platform can keep evolving: how to keep decisions coherent across regions and partners, how to maintain trust in data as the landscape changes, and how to adopt innovation without accumulating risk or complexity that slows the business down later.

For many senior HR leaders, this tension becomes most visible at the point where operational decisions begin to carry strategic weight. Choices about data models, process design, governance, or AI adoption no longer affect just HR efficiency; they shape how confidently the organisation can plan, respond, and grow. At that level, HR technology stops being a delivery concern and becomes a question of organisational capability.

That shift changes the role SAP SuccessFactors plays in the enterprise, and it changes what leaders need from the engagement model around it.

From HR Delivery to Strategic Enablement

At leadership level, HR technology decisions quickly move beyond implementation performance. The focus shifts to organisational capability: data quality leaders can trust, processes that scale across geographies, governance models that survive organisational change, and an operating model that can absorb innovation without disruption.

SAP SuccessFactors plays a central role in this shift, but senior HR leaders know that technology alone does not create capability. What matters just as much is how decisions are made, how trade‑offs are managed, and how intent is preserved as complexity grows.

Once HR technology becomes a strategic enabler rather than a delivery concern, leadership attention naturally shifts toward the areas where coherence is hardest to maintain over time. The visual below reflects three priorities that senior HR leaders consistently return to - not because they are new, but because they are the hardest to sustain as complexity, scale, and ambition increase.

What Max Success Plan provides is not a different set of goals, but a way to operationalise these priorities continuously: through governance, decision support, and expert safeguarding as the organisation evolves.

Harmonising Workforce Data and People Processes

For senior leaders, workforce data is no longer an operational output; it is a strategic asset. It underpins business planning, regulatory confidence, workforce decisions, and executive credibility. When data is inconsistent or fragmented, leaders feel the impact directly: through slower decisions, reduced confidence in analytics, and increased risk exposure.

In complex organisations, data fragmentation is rarely the result of poor intent. It emerges organically from legitimate local optimisation, phased rollouts, acquisitions, regional requirements, and evolving processes. Over time, however, these variations accumulate, and the organisation loses a shared understanding of what the data represents and how it should be used.

Max Success Plan addresses this challenge by safeguarding transformation design, architecture governance, and data and analytics adoption across the lifecycle. The focus is not on enforcing uniformity, but on ensuring that variation is intentional, explainable, and governable. This allows leaders to maintain a trusted data foundation, one they can rely on not just for reporting, but for strategic decisions that affect the entire workforce.

Enhancing HR Operations with AI - Responsibly

You already know this - AI has introduced a new level of urgency into HR leadership conversations. Senior HR leaders are expected to leverage AI to improve efficiency, insight, and employee experience, while simultaneously ensuring ethical use, transparency, compliance, and organisational readiness.

From a leadership perspective, the challenge is not access to AI capabilities. It is knowing when and how to deploy them responsibly at scale. Architectural dependencies, data readiness, governance models, and change maturity all determine whether AI becomes a strategic accelerator, or a source of unmanaged risk.

Max Success Plan supports leaders by combining architecture transformation, continuous innovation guidance, and business transformation management. This enables organisations to move beyond isolated experimentation and towards structured, value‑driven AI adoption. The result is not slower innovation, but innovation that enhances HR operations without undermining trust or stability.

Enabling Informed Workforce Decisions at Scale

As HR becomes more analytical, senior leaders are judged less on insight availability and more on decision quality. Having dashboards and data is no longer enough; leaders need confidence that insights are consistent, comparable, and actionable across the organisation.

This is particularly challenging in environments where systems, teams, and priorities continue to change. Without ongoing alignment, analytics frameworks drift, definitions diverge, and insights lose their strategic value.

Max Success Plan supports this priority by providing continuous evolution guidance and transformation safeguarding. This ensures that analytics adoption, decision frameworks, and governance remain aligned with the organisation’s operating model as it evolves. For leaders, this means they can act with confidence, even as complexity increases, knowing that the foundations supporting their decisions are being actively maintained.

Taken together, these priorities - trusted workforce data, responsible AI adoption, and decision‑quality at scale - point to something broader than operational excellence. They describe a leadership responsibility: ensuring that HR transformation remains aligned with enterprise ambition as complexity, pace, and expectation continue to increase.

This is where Max Success Plan moves beyond delivery support and becomes a distinct leadership instrument.

Why Max Is a Leadership Instrument, Not an IT Upgrade

At executive level, what distinguishes Max Success Plan is not the breadth of services it includes, but the nature of the questions it is designed to support.

Max exists to safeguard strategic intent across time, scale, and organisational change. It provides expert oversight at moments where decisions have long‑term implications: choices around architecture, data models, integration strategy, governance structures, extensibility, and AI adoption - often well before the consequences of those decisions become visible.

For senior HR leaders, this creates a different operating dynamic: instead of intervening reactively to correct drift or resolve misalignment after the fact, leaders gain confidence that the transformation is being continuously interpreted and aligned to strategic objectives as it evolves. The emphasis shifts from course‑correction to deliberate steering.

How Leaders Experience the Difference

Leaders who operate within a Max Success Plan engagement often describe a noticeable shift in where their time and attention are spent: there is less need to arbitrate architectural or governance debates once decisions are already embedded. Less effort is required to reconcile divergent interpretations of earlier design choices. Instead, conversations move earlier in the decision cycle and focus increasingly on future capability, organisational readiness, and opportunity.

This change is enabled by the presence of an independent, experienced layer of oversight whose sole responsibility is to safeguard the transformation as a whole, rather than individual initiatives, teams, or partners. That independence allows guidance to remain anchored to long‑term intent, even as priorities, stakeholders, and timelines change.

For leadership, this matters because it restores confidence in the transformation itself, not just in its delivery.

The Max Success Plan conversation typically begins when complexity is no longer something the organisation simply manages, but something that begins to shape leadership risk. This often occurs when SAP SuccessFactors underpins strategic workforce initiatives, when AI and analytics influence executive‑level decisions, or when the organisation’s ability to adapt becomes visibly linked to the coherence and credibility of its HR landscape.

At that point, the central question is no longer whether SAP SuccessFactors can support the organisation. It is whether the transformation around it is being actively guided with the same level of care and intent as the business strategy it enables. That is the role Max Success Plan is designed to play.

The next step is not a technical review, but a conversation with your Customer Success Manager or SAP Account Executive about whether your organisation would benefit from that level of partnership as it continues to evolve.

entry.title: Re: Response Header validation Error in SAP CPI
entry.content: Please ask the API provider to correct or remove the header by ensuring it does not contain leading spaces, tabs, newlines, or multiline values, and that it follows RFC compliant formatting.
